author: v*****a
comment: Beauty Effect:The finish is a gorgeous satin-matte or what some might call a "soft-focus" matte. It’s not flat or drying like some matte foundations, but it's also not overly dewy, which is a major plus for our climate. It gives the skin a healthy, perfected, and smooth appearance that looks surprisingly skin-like. Beauty Profile:Skin Type: Combination (oily T-zone, normal cheeks) Skin Tone: Light-medium with warm, slightly golden undertones. Concerns: Redness around the nose, some post-acne marks, and visible pores. Packaging:The cushion comes in a sleek, metallic silver compact that feels sturdy and looks more premium than its price point suggests. Inside, you'll find a good-sized mirror and the signature teardrop-shaped puff, which is genuinely fantastic for getting into the corners of the nose and under the eyes. The puff is soft and dense, picking up the perfect amount of product.  As someone living in the perpetual heat and humidity place, finding a cushion foundation that delivers on coverage without melting off or feeling like a heavy mask is a constant challenge. Shade 03A Almond is a beautiful match for light-to-medium skin with warm or neutral-warm undertones. It doesn’t pull too yellow or too pink, striking a nice, natural balance. Upon initial application, the shade was a perfect fit for my skin tone. The formula is incredibly lightweight and spreads with ease. A few gentle taps with the puff are enough to cover a large area. It blends seamlessly into the skin without needing much effort. ​With just one light layer, it provided solid medium coverage, effectively evening out my skin tone and toning down the redness around my nose. It’s also very buildable. I added a second, thinner layer over some darker spots, and it built up to near-full coverage without looking cakey or thick.
